Stage 4 done: WITH chaining + UNION. Restructured query.Query into Branches(UNION)->Parts(WITH) with UnionKind enum; per-part scope threading (exported names L-to-R, WITH * transitive, cross-branch isolation); parameters stay query-wide. godog read-core 371 passed / 179 pending (was 336/173 at Stage 3 end; +35 scenarios parse, clauses/with+clauses/union added to readCoreDirs). 7 WITH/UNION negatives skiplisted (all verified below the type-interface boundary by adversarial review). Linus gate APPROVED. Commits 4491fb8(model)+b6cd944(unlock)+5821e4c(prep) pushed to origin/stage-4-with-union. NO PR per loop discipline.

Stage 5 complete: undirected relationship patterns (a)-[r]-(b) and (a)<-[r]->(b) now parse. EdgeBinding gains a positive 'directed' marker (field+Directed() accessor+trailing constructor param+always-emitted JSON key); listener uses an inverted zero-value-safe 'undirected' raw field, polarity flip at toBinding. collectEdge stops rejecting left==right; directed edges canonicalise, undirected keep textual order (no flip; <--> guarded). godog read-core 371/179 -> 380/170 (+9 passed). Linus gate APPROVED. Branch stage-5-undirected pushed to origin (commits dae7fb3 prep, b474fbe model, d4fbedd unlock). Not N/A: parser is schema-agnostic, marker is load-bearing (undirected yields no single EdgeKey, resolver forms two candidate keys).

Freeze landed: PR #81 merged as 6bdb86c (closes GH #15). ADR 0008 declares query.Query frozen, inventories the frozen shape (sums: Binding x5, PathMember x4, Endpoint x2, Projection x5, Type x17, Use x3, Effect x8, SetEffect x3 + all axes), pins the resolver API (internal/resolver; resolver.New(schema, opts...) + WithRegistry(procsig.Registry); pure Resolve(query.Query) -> (ValidatedQuery, error)) discharging ADR 0006's deferral, and defines the additive-only revision protocol + apidiff gate. ADR 0003 header flipped to frozen note; CONTEXT.md Resolver/Validated-query entries updated. Linus adversarial review: round 1 FAIL (sentinel-retirement overreach, fixed in 79c50b0) -> round 2 PASS zero blockers, all claims verified first-party incl. godog baseline 3897/3459/438/0.

Epic complete: all pre-freeze scope delivered. Stages 6-14 (gqlc-97z, 9b4, 9nl, ydk, 2av, 665, mkv, cqh, im1) + scope ADR gqlc-39s + TCK sweep gqlc-x6u + pre-freeze fixes gqlc-33k.1/.2 + gqlc-3r0 all closed; freeze gqlc-cta landed as PR #81 (6bdb86c, ADR 0008). Remaining open children re-homed: 33k.3/.4 -> gqlc-0mx (post-freeze, consumer-driven); gqlc-c5f re-scoped standalone (premise corrected: Neo4j dialect extension, not a grammar bug); gqlc-7ur detached (standalone P3 polish).

The dependency edge on gqlc-0mx (resolver/codegen) was removed at close: that is post-freeze work this umbrella was never scoped to contain — it proceeds under gqlc-0mx directly. Superseded by R5 spec B7 resolution (PR #101). The contract recorded here (resolver-side re-parse of ExprProjection text to detect nested aggregates) was retracted: the frozen model drops nested-aggregate structure at classification (classifyRichExpression returns plain ExprProjection; recovered text re-parses opaque, so re-parse cannot recover the aggregate bit). R5 landed the uniform-exclude posture instead: ExprProjection items are NEVER grouping keys; result-set effect is under-grouping when a Part mixes AggregateProjection with ExprProjection. Parser pin 'count in arithmetic' (parser_test.go:1319) is the explicit deferral lock. Escape hatch: Shape B ContainsAggregate model-unfreeze follow-up bead (parser-side bit set by classifyRichExpression during its own walk) filed for owner decision per ADR 0008 revision protocol.

Won't do (owner decision, 2026-07-10). shortestPath()/allShortestPaths() are Neo4j dialect extensions with zero rules in the official openCypher grammar (verified against internal/grammar/cypher/Cypher.g4) and zero occurrences in the vendored TCK. The freeze (ADR 0008) already recorded them as out-of-scope; the owner has elected not to add the dialect layer / model axis / parser lowering that would be needed. Not superseded — dropped entirely. Reopen if a future consumer requires Neo4j-dialect parity.
